discovery.run_summary: Risk-off tape with SPY down sharply, KRE leading losses, and mega-cap tech weak (ORCL -5.8%, NVDA -3.6%, MSFT -3.2%) — AVGO post-earnings -14% headline weighing on semis sentiment despite SMH up on the day. Energy and healthcare bucking the trend. Defense names selling off hard with no obvious company-specific catalyst, suggesting sector rotation.

notable_index_moves: KRE -2.4% leading sector weakness — regional bank stress signal worth watching
XLE +1.3% and XLV +0.8% defensive/energy bid vs broad tape weakness
ITA -1.5% defense ETF down, consistent with KTOS/AVAV sell-off
VIX +1.8% but still subdued at 16 — orderly de-risking, not panic

discoveries.name: Alaska Air Group, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Officer departure 8-K combined with extended multi-day decline in an airline
discoveries.thesis: ALK has now dropped sharply over five days and added another leg lower on a same-day 8-K disclosing officer departure plus Reg FD. CFO/operational departures mid-quarter at airlines tend to precede guidance issues; combined with the prior week's slide, the market may still be underpricing follow-through if the departure is abrupt rather than planned retirement. Small-cap airline at ~$4.7B with no buffer.
discoveries.what_confirms: 8-K exhibit reveals CFO or COO departure without successor named, or Reg FD disclosure flags soft RASM/fuel-cost guidance; analyst downgrades follow within 48 hours
discoveries.what_kills: Departure is a planned CEO/board retirement with successor in place, or Reg FD item is a routine investor conference deck with no negative content — then today's drop is the capitulation low
discoveries.what_to_learn: 5.02 + 7.01 same-day filings at airlines/transports during a multi-day decline often signal management is getting ahead of a guide-down; the Reg FD item is the tell
discoveries.catalyst: 8-K filed 2026-06-03 with officer departure (5.02) and Reg FD disclosure (7.01)
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Multi-item 8-K with 5.02 + 7.01 same day, filed into an already-declining stock — classic bad-news bundling pattern
research_pointers: Read the actual 8-K exhibit to identify WHO departed and whether successor was named
Check if Reg FD item contains an investor presentation or guidance update
Compare to UAL/DAL/AAL action today to separate ALK-specific from sector
Look at June traffic data and fuel cost trajectory

discoveries.name: Kratos Defense & Security Solutions
discoveries.setup: Defense small/mid-cap selling off hard with no company-specific 8-K, on a down ITA day
discoveries.thesis: KTOS dropping sharply on average volume with no SEC filing and ITA only down ~1.5% looks like a sympathy/momentum unwind rather than fundamental news. Defense names had run hard; today's risk-off + AVGO headline-driven tech weakness is dragging high-beta growth-defense names disproportionately. AVAV showing the same pattern (-6.3% on no catalyst, similar profile) supports the read that this is a basket de-grossing event.
discoveries.what_confirms: No 8-K appears in next 24 hours, no analyst downgrade surfaces, KTOS/AVAV trade together going forward suggesting paired exposure unwind
discoveries.what_kills: A program loss, DoD budget headline, or competitor contract win emerges to explain the magnitude; or an 8-K filing appears overnight
discoveries.what_to_learn: When two same-industry mid-caps drop ~6-8% on the same day with no filings and average volume, suspect a basket/factor unwind rather than fundamentals — these tend to retrace partially within days
discoveries.catalyst: No company-specific catalyst identified; sector-wide defense weakness with ITA -1.5%
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: catalyst_signals is empty; volume_multiple at 0.99 (not a panic-volume event); AVAV showing identical pattern
research_pointers: Check if there is a defense industry note from a major broker today
Look at quant factor performance — high-beta growth vs low-vol on the day
Verify no DoD program/budget news on Kratos UAV programs
Cross-reference with PLTR, RKLB, LMT moves for pattern

discoveries.name: GameStop Corporation
discoveries.setup: Earnings reaction with 2.02 + 7.01 filing on a heavily-meme name
discoveries.thesis: GME up ~6% on 1.88x volume the day after an earnings 8-K. Cannot tell from filing codes alone whether print was actually good or whether this is retail/meme positioning. GME's price action has been decoupled from fundamentals for years; without reading the actual release, a directional call past 'reacted positively' would be guessing.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Multi-item 8-K with earnings + Reg FD; market reaction is positive but GME-specific dynamics (cash hoard, BTC strategy speculation, retail flow) dominate fundamentals
research_pointers: Read the actual earnings release and Reg FD content
Check whether any commentary on bitcoin/treasury strategy was included
Look at options flow for unusual size

discoveries.name: Exelixis, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Mid-cap biotech rallying on a healthcare-strong tape (XBI +1.6%, XLV +0.8%)
discoveries.thesis: EXEL +6.3% on below-average volume in a strong biotech tape. The 5.02 + 5.07 8-K is dated 5/29 (annual meeting director slate — routine), not today's catalyst. Move likely reflects XBI strength plus a possible clinical/pipeline data point not visible here. Magnitude is in line with quality biotech moving with a hot XBI day.
discoveries.catalyst: Healthcare/biotech sector strength; 5/29 8-K appears to be annual meeting (5.07) and director election (5.02), not today's driver
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 5.07 code = shareholder vote results, typically annual meeting; combined with 5.02 same-day = director election results — routine governance, not the catalyst for today's move
research_pointers: Check ASCO 2026 abstract release schedule — early June is peak oncology conference window
Compare to other oncology mid-caps moving today

catalyst_chains.primary_event: AVGO post-earnings -14% on weak software sales and unchanged AI chip forecast

ai_announcements.headline: Alphabet raises record $85B for AI business
ai_announcements.summary: Alphabet's record stock sale for AI investments signals strong investor appetite for AI capex.

ai_announcements.headline: Amazon to show AI-generated product images in search
ai_announcements.summary: Amazon will use visual search and AI to display AI-generated product images matching user queries.

ai_announcements.headline: UK regulators force Google to offer publisher opt-out from AI Search
ai_announcements.summary: Regulation requires Google to offer publishers an opt-out from generative AI search features, testing in UK then rolling out globally.

ai_announcements.headline: Meta's WhatsApp Business AI agent launches globally with token-based pricing
ai_announcements.summary: Meta is monetizing WhatsApp Business via an AI agent priced per token.

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: None of the moving stocks in market_data have plausible direct linkage to today's AI announcements. KTOS/AVAV (-7.6%/-6.3%) are defense names — likely a defense-sector specific catalyst, not AI-related; no AI announcement here touches drones/defense autonomy. GME +6% follows its own 8-K earnings filing. EXEL/AXSM moves are biotech-specific. ALK -4.6% is airline-specific with an officer-change 8-K. Alphabet's $85B raise and Amazon's AI search image feature are the most market-relevant items but no listed ticker shows a clean reaction (e.g., no ad-tech, no e-commerce names in the move list). Quiet night for AI-driven mispricing among the names provided.
